name: Eonavian (Galician-Asturian, gallego-asturiano)

ancestors.notes: Galician / Galaico-Portuguese stock: Eonavian (the speech of the Navia-Eo region, western Asturias, between the río Navia and the río Eo) descends genetically from the same Galaico-Portuguese continuum as Galician, sharing its defining western-Ibero-Romance outcomes: Latin PL-/CL-/FL- → [tʃ] (chover, chave, chamar — not Asturian ll-), Latin -CT-/-ULT- → [it] (feito, muito), no diphthongisation of Latin Ĕ/Ŏ (terra, porta — not Asturian tierra, puerta), ⟨x⟩ = [ʃ], falling diphthongs ou/ei preserved. Galician-side scholarship (RAG, ILG, Fernández Rei) classifies it as 'galego de Asturias'.

Western Asturian (Asturleonese) adstrate — the reason the ALLA (Academia de la Llingua Asturiana) names the variety 'gallego-asturiano / eonaviego' and treats it as a transitional speech of its own. Asturian contact features: raising of final unstressed -o → [-u] and -e → [-i] in the easternmost parishes, spread of the neuter-of-matter agreement, some lexical and morphological Asturianisms, and orthographic conventions of the ALLA's own 'Normes ortográfiques'. The east↔west gradient within Navia-Eo runs from more Asturian-like (Navia) to more Galician-like (Eo).
Gallaecian Celtic substrate of the northwest, inherited through the Galaico-Portuguese stock.

notes: Eonavian (eonaviego, gallego-asturiano, fala del Navia-Eo) — the Galician-Portuguese transitional speech of the Navia-Eo comarca in westernmost Asturias (Spain), between the río Eo (border with Galicia) and the río Navia. CODE CHOICE: modelled under the GALICIAN clade as gl-x-eonavia rather than under Asturian, because its structural phonology is Galaico-Portuguese (PL-/CL-/FL- → [tʃ], -CT- → [it], no Ĕ/Ŏ diphthongisation), which is the genetic-classification criterion the o2i graph already applies (gl.json and gl-x-oriental.json both name 'A Fala, Eonaviego' as the Galician-Asturian transitional zone). This is a deliberate, literature-grounded choice on a genuinely contested variety: the ALLA calls it 'gallego-asturiano' and administers it as an Asturian regional speech, while Galician institutions (RAG/ILG) call it 'galego de Asturias'; both agree it is Galician-Portuguese in origin with heavy Asturian adstrate, which the ancestry block encodes (parent gl weight 0.62, ast-x-occidental adstrate 0.28). KEY FEATURES modelled as deltas over gl: (1) ⟨ch⟩ = [tʃ] robustly PRESERVED word-initially and intervocalically (chover, cheo) — no urban deaffrication to [ʃ]. (2) ⟨ll⟩ = [ʎ] (palatal lateral retained; no yeísmo). (3) ⟨x⟩ = [ʃ]. FEATURES SHARED with the gl base and therefore inherited, not re-declared: seven tonic vowels /a e ɛ i o ɔ u/; apico-alveolar /s̺/; betacism ⟨b⟩=⟨v⟩=/b/; intervocalic voiced-stop spirantisation β/ð/ɣ; falling diphthongs ou/ei. DIALECT-INTERNAL GRADIENT (documented, not phoneme-split here): the easternmost parishes raise final -o/-e to [-u]/[-i] and show more Asturian traits; distinción (/θ/) is general, with pockets of seseo and gheada near the Galician side. Provenance honesty: base phonology inherited from the research-tier gl spec; the Eonavian-specific deltas are page-pinned to the ALLA Normas Fonética section (§1.1-1.3) and Frías Conde (Ianua 5, pp.93-106), both read directly.

sources.author: Academia de la Llingua Asturiana (Secretaría Llingüística del Navia-Eo)
sources.title: Normas ortográficas del gallego-asturiano
sources.notes: Read directly (papers/iberian/alla_normas_ortograficas_gallego_asturiano.pdf). §1.1 alphabet/consonant inventory /a b θ k tʃ d e ɛ f g i l ʎ m n ɲ o ɔ p ɾ r s t u/ plus /ʃ ʝ/. §1.2.2 /θ/ is a full phoneme (distinción; NO gheada, /g/ plain). §1.2.4 Latin -L-/-LL- → /ʎ/ in the eastern zone (lleite, llobo) vs /l/ in the west (leite) → the (l)l convention. §1.2.5 -LJ-/-K'L- → /ʎ/ everywhere (muller, ollo); /ʝ/ (y) with variable ʎ~ʝ merger. §1.2.6 native ⟨x⟩ = [ʃ] (xabón, queixo); Castilian jota /x/ not native. §1.3.1/§1.3.3 seven-vowel tonic system with /e ɛ/ and /o ɔ/. The variety's orthography is thus ALLA-administered even though its phonology is Galaico-Portuguese.

sources.author: Frías Conde, X.
sources.title: O relativo do continuum entre galego e asturiano en Asturias
sources.notes: Read directly (papers/iberian/frias_conde_continuum_ianua05.pdf). The classification argument: rebuts García Arias point-by-point and affirms the galeguidade of eonaviego. p.96-97: the west→east -LL- gradient (leite > lleite > yeite) is argued as an ancient isogloss, not Asturian influence; native metaphony equa>eugua; characteristic intervocalic -n- denasalisation (farina>faría, miña>mía). Vocalism + nasality are the strongest Galaico-Portuguese diagnostics.

sources.author: Nieto Rodríguez, S.
sources.title: El gallego-asturiano (TFG)
sources.notes: Read (papers/iberian/nieto_tfg_gallego_asturiano_uniovi.pdf). Uniovi institutional-repository overview of the variety.

sources.author: Fernández Rei, F.
sources.title: Dialectoloxía da lingua galega
sources.notes: Classifies the Navia-Eo speech within the eastern Galician block ('galego de Asturias'). Book, not open — cited via the OA Frías Conde and Dubert sources.

location.notes: Navia-Eo comarca, westernmost Asturias, between the río Eo and the río Navia (approx. Vegadeo/A Veiga).
